questions.text: SONAR is mostly used by
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Navigators**

SONAR (Sound Navigation and Ranging) uses ultrasonic waves to detect underwater objects.

Option Analysis:
• Astronauts → Work in space, not underwater detection.
• Doctors → Use ultrasound imaging, not SONAR navigation.
• Engineers → May design SONAR but do not primarily use it.
• Navigators → Correct. Used in submarines and ships to detect objects and measure ocean depth.

questions.text: Which of the following is measured by ‘Anemometer’?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Force of the wind**

An anemometer measures **wind speed or wind force**.

Option Analysis:
• Velocity of water flow → Measured by flow meter.
• Depth of water → Measured by fathometer.
• Force of wind → Correct.
• Intensity of light → Measured by luxmeter.

questions.text: The velocity of wind is measured by
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Anemometer**

Wind speed measurement is an important parameter in meteorology.

Option Analysis:
• Barometer → Measures atmospheric pressure.
• Anemometer → Correct.
• Hydrometer → Measures density of liquids.
• Wind vane → Measures wind direction, not velocity.

questions.text: Which one of the following is not correctly matched?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Tacheometer - Pressure difference**

A tacheometer measures **distance or horizontal/vertical angles in surveying**, not pressure.

questions.text: Pyrometer is used to measure
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: High temperature**

Pyrometers measure temperatures above **700°C**, often used in furnaces and metallurgy.

questions.text: Which one of the following devices is used to measure extremely high temperature?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Pyrometer**

Used in industries such as **steel production and ceramics**.

questions.text: Which one of the following thermometers is known as pyrometer?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Radiation thermometer**

Radiation thermometers measure temperature based on emitted thermal radiation.

questions.text: The thermometer which used to measure 2000ºC temperature is
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Total radiation pyrometer**

Mercury thermometers cannot measure very high temperatures.

questions.text: ‘Pyrheliometer’ is used for measuring
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Solar radiation**

Pyrheliometers measure **direct beam solar radiation**.

questions.text: What is measured by manometer?
options: Pressure of oil on the surface
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Pressure of gas**

Manometers measure pressure difference between gases.

questions.text: Which instrument is used to measure atmospheric pressure?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Barometer**

Barometers measure **atmospheric pressure**, important in weather prediction.

questions.text: The liquid/liquids used in the simple barometer is/are
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Mercury**

Mercury is used due to **high density and low vapour pressure**.

questions.text: The density of milk can be obtained by the use of
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Lactometer**

A lactometer determines **purity of milk by density measurement**.

questions.text: Which instrument is used for measuring humidity in the air?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Hygrometer**

Humidity is the **amount of water vapour in air**.

questions.text: Hygrometer is used to measure
questions.solution: **Correct Answer: Humidity in atmosphere**

questions.text: Which of the following is correctly matched?
questions.solution: **Correct Answer: Thermoresistor – Electronic thermometer**

questions.text: The name of the equipment used for measuring blood pressure is
questions.solution: **Correct Answer: Sphygmomanometer**

questions.text: Which one of the following can be used to measure temperature above 1500ºC?
questions.solution: **Correct Answer: Pyrometer**

questions.text: The apparatus used to measure the intensity of light is known as
questions.solution: **Correct Answer: Luxmeter**

questions.text: Radar is used for
options: Detecting objects by using light waves
Reflecting sound waves to detect objects
Determining the presence and location of objects with radio wave
questions.solution: **Correct Answer: Determining the presence and location of objects with radio wave**
